description: Negative-consent model. Each session the AI plans the next 12 hours, emails a reviewable plan, and executes all safe, semi-safe, and reversible high-risk work automatically unless the owner stops or modifies a specific item via Telegram. Only irreversible/destructive task types in 'require_explicit_approval' need a positive go-ahead.

review_model.auto_execute: Everything listed in the emailed plan runs automatically during the session window unless the owner intervenes via Telegram.
owner_controls: stop <TASK_ID> â€” cancel a planned or running item
change <TASK_ID> to <instruction> â€” modify an item before it runs
approve <TASK_ID> â€” green-light an item in require_explicit_approval
pause â€” halt all autonomous execution until resumed
review_model.plan_email: Sent at the start of each session (8:00 AM and 8:00 PM {{TIMEZONE_ABBR}}), listing everything that will run in the next 12 hours.

require_explicit_approval_note: Irreversible or destructive. These NEVER auto-run under the opt-out model â€” they are listed under 'needs_explicit_go' in the plan email and require an explicit 'approve <TASK_ID>' via Telegram (high-risk pipeline Phase 1). Reversible high-impact changes (homepage_edit, navigation_change, redirect_setup, new_service_page, schema_removal) ARE auto-approved: the pipelines publish them straight to production (main), the owner reviews the live site daily before search engines index, and deploy-rollback reverts anything unwanted. There is NO preview/human-merge gate â€” do not plan work that depends on one.

execution_routing.note: Two consumer lanes execute approved work; the planner assigns risk_level/task_type so each task lands in a lane that can actually finish it. NEVER create work that has no finishing executor (that is what caused empty preview branches + zombie 'preview_validated' tasks).

deterministic_ops.deploys: straight to production via safe-fix (safe and semi_safe both â†’ safe-fix --production)
deterministic_ops.handles_note: ranking_followup is self-scheduled by the executor after a ranking-affecting deploy (deferred ~14d via scheduled_for); it re-checks SERP positions and enqueues ranking_recovery + an alert on a drop. The planner does not need to create these.
deterministic_ops.risk_guidance: Assign these risk_level=safe (or semi_safe). Both auto-deploy to production with no gate.

hermes_content.deploys: straight to production (main) via a fresh Hermes authoring/refresh session
handles: service_page_gap + money_page_refresh (edit_refresh_needed â€” rewrites/fixes an EXISTING page in place)
hermes_content.risk_guidance: Any task that REWRITES or AUTHORS page copy is a content task â€” route it by task_type to this lane. Do NOT mark page-rewrite work as a generic high_risk ops task; the ops safe executor has no content handler and the high-risk pipeline will mark it 'blocked'.

explicit_approval.flow: high-risk Phase 1 â†’ waiting_for_approval â†’ owner 'approve <id>' via Telegram

homepage_canonical_money_terms.rule: The HOMEPAGE is the canonical ranking target for these head money terms. Do NOT create or push dedicated /services/* pages for them â€” that cannibalizes the homepage. Support the homepage instead: (1) risk=safe internal_link_opportunity tasks linking these anchors from topically-relevant blogs to the homepage, and (2) supporting blog posts (new_blog_post) that link up to the homepage. The keyword registry already maps all six to the homepage (page_type=home).

explicit_approval_queue.mechanism: high-risk pipeline Phase 1 (v2 task approve request)
explicit_approval_queue.description: Only require_explicit_approval task types are held here. They appear under 'needs_explicit_go' in the plan email and proceed only when the owner approves via Telegram. All other work proceeds automatically.
